title: EPS 2026: New withdrawal rules and 20-day claim settlement explained
summary: With EPS 2026, the pension system for organized sector employees is reformed for transparency and efficiency. It mandates a 36-month wait for withdrawal benefits, enforces a 20-day claim processing deadline, and supports higher pensions, while preserving existing benefits for current members.

title: If you invested like Trump, you'd be ahead of the S&P 500 — here's what the US President is betting on
summary: Trump's financial disclosures reveal he executed 21,285 stock trades in 2025, averaging one trade every five minutes. His portfolio has outperformed the S&amp;P 500, with a 37.3% return since his inauguration. Here's a look at the US leader's portfolio .

summary: The EPFO has restored its passbook portal after a two-week outage due to a database upgrade. Members can access their accounts again ahead of 8.25% interest credit.&nbsp;

title: Same salary, different loan amount? Here's why banks may treat borrowers differently
summary: Lenders consider factors beyond income when approving loans, such as repayment history and financial habits. Two individuals with identical salaries may experience different loan outcomes based on their overall financial profiles.

title: Which ITR form should you file? A guide for salaried employees, pensioners and other taxpayers
summary: The ITR forms have been revised for AY 2026-27, affecting ITR-1, ITR-4 and ITR-2. Taxpayers must ensure correct filing by reviewing income sources and changes to avoid complications. The filing deadline is 31 July 2026 for individuals and non-audited entities.
